data.title: Goldman Sachs Names 3 'Strong Buy' Energy Stocks with Room to Run
data.description:
A mix of factors has lifted oil prices after they hit a 12-month low in December. Heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East – particularly concerns about supply risks linked to Iran – have supported crude benchmarks, as well as ongoing uncertainty around production from sanctioned exporters such as Venezuela. Broader shifts in market sentiment tied to U.S. policy rhetoric and risk appetite have also contributed to volatility in energy markets.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Any sustained rise in oil prices would ripple through the broader economy. However, senior energy analysts at Goldman Sachs argue that volatility can also create opportunity. In a recent note, the team highlighted three energy stocks they believe are well positioned in the current environment.

Goldman's selections span multiple segments of the energy complex, including exploration and production, midstream infrastructure, and utilities, reinforcing the case for diversification.

Each stock also carries a Strong Buy consensus rating on Wall Street, according to TipRanks data. With that context in mind, let's take a closer look at what sets these names apart.

Viper Energy (VNOM)

Goldman's exploration and production (E&P) pick is Viper energy, a limited partnership firm that owns and exploits oil and natural gas assets in North America. The company acquires assets in the form of mineral rights and royalty interests, located in high-margin hydrocarbon areas that are mainly undeveloped. Viper's business model is designed to sustain free cash flow without requiring high demands on capital.

Viper is a subsidiary of the Texas-based E&P firm Diamondback Energy, and uses that relationship to inform its purchases of assets, particularly royalty interests. Diamondback, in turn, is the primary operator on Viper's assets, and has a clear incentive to develop those acres for improved consolidated returns. The mutual relationship benefits both companies – and Viper's shareholders.

The shareholders gain because they are the 'primary business' for Viper. The company has prioritized providing attractive shareholder returns as a business goal, which it does by loading up on mineral and royalty assets in the Permian basin, one of Texas' richest hydrocarbon-bearing formations.

Investors should be aware of a couple of important developments with Viper in recent months. In August of last year, the company acquired Sitio Royalties in an all-equity transaction that brought Sitio's assets under Viper's rubric. The new assets were integrated into Viper's operations during 3Q25.

This was followed in November by the announcement that Viper had entered into an agreement to sell off its non-Permian assets to an affiliate of GRP Energy Capital and Warwick Capital Partners. This transaction was valued at $670 million, and is expected to close during 1Q26.

Viper pays out a 33-cent regular dividend to shareholders, with the last payment sent out on November 20. It was accompanied by a 25-cent variable dividend – Viper has a history in recent years of adding variable dividends to the regular payment. The regular dividend annualizes to $1.32 per common share and gives a 3% forward yield, while the addition of the variable increased the yield to 5.2%.

In 3Q25, the last period reported, Viper realized an adjusted net income of $0.40 per share, beating the forecast by a penny. The company finished Q3 with $53 million in cash and liquid assets on hand.

For Goldman's Neil Mehta, an expert on the energy industry, Viper presents a combination of sound valuation and a positive outlook going forward. He writes of the company, "We see VNOM as a stock with positive valuation support given shares trade at a 10% FCF yield on our 2027 estimates (using $70/bbl Brent oil) – a modest premium relative to oily E&P peer average of 11%. We continue to highlight the benefits of VNOM's no-capex royalty business model which supports VNOM's meaningful return of capital commitments (at least 75% of cash available for distribution to shareholders) and low dividend breakeven of ~$30/bbl WTI. While the announced non-Permian assets sales are still pending close, we remain constructive on the long-term benefits stemming from the close relationship between VNOM and FANG as well as the shareholder return profile of the pro-forma, pure-play Permian business in an uncertain oil environment."

Mehta, a 5-star analyst, puts a Buy rating on VNOM along with a $54 price target that points toward a one-year upside potential of 22%. (To watch Mehta's track record, click here)

The Strong Buy consensus rating on VNOM shares is unanimous, based on 11 recent positive analyst reviews. The stock is trading for $44.25 and its $50.8 average target price implies a gain of 15% in the coming year. (See VNOM stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Strategy Stock Posted that it "Can Withstand a Drawdown in $BTC Price to $8K" as Michael Saylor Doubles Down on His Never Sell Mantra
data.description:
Michael Saylor is standing firm as he addresses fears that his company might be forced to dump its massive Bitcoin (BTC-USD) stash. Saylor recently re-posted a big update from Strategy (MSTR) on X. The company posted that "Strategy can withstand a drawdown in $BTC price to $8K and still have sufficient assets to fully cover our debt." Even at that low level, the company says its assets would still be enough to fully pay off its roughly $6 billion in debt. While the market has been volatile, Saylor is doubling down on his never-sell mantra.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Strategy's Plan Involves Turning Debt into Stock

During a recent appearance on CNBC, Saylor explained that the company is moving toward a strategy called "equitizing" its debt. Instead of worrying about how to pay back billions in cash when loans come due between 2027 and 2032, Strategy plans to gradually convert those loans into company stock. Saylor noted that the company has no interest in getting rid of its digital assets, stating clearly, "We're not going to be selling, we're going to be buying Bitcoin. I expect we'll be buying Bitcoin every quarter forever." He believes this shift away from traditional debt will make the company a secure financial vault that doesn't have to worry about the typical risks of a bank loan.

Why Saylor Isn't Worried about a Crash

Saylor is often asked what happens if the market stays down for years. He told investors that the company has about two and a half years' worth of cash just sitting on its balance sheet to cover interest and dividend payments. "If Bitcoin falls 90% for the next four years, we'll refinance the debt," Saylor said, adding that the company would simply "roll it forward" rather than panic. He views Bitcoin as a long-term asset that will eventually outperform everything else, and he believes lenders will continue to support the company because of that long-term value.

Critics Warn of a Dump on Regular Investors

Despite Saylor's confidence, not everyone thinks this is a clever move. Some analysts argue that turning debt into equity is just a fancy way of diluting the stock. When a company equitizes its debt, it creates new shares, which can make the shares current investors own less valuable. Critics like Anton Golub have gone as far as to call this a planned "dump on retail investors," suggesting that the company is effectively shifting its risk onto everyday people. Skeptics also point out that if Bitcoin really hit $8,000, Strategy would be sitting on a massive paper loss of nearly $48 billion, which could make it very hard to find new lenders willing to help them refinance.

Is Strategy Inc a Buy or Sell?

According to TipRanks, MSTR stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on a unanimous 11 Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month MSTR price target of $359.82 implies 169% upside from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: DraftKings Stock (DKNG) Plunge 'Should Be at an End,' Jefferies Says after Earnings Rout
data.description:
Wall Street has greeted DraftKings' (DKNG) below-consensus guidance for fiscal 2026 with price target cuts; however, Jefferies (JEF) believes the downward trend in the company's estimates and shares "should be at an end." 

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On Friday, DraftKings' shares sank 13.51% after the Boston-based company posted fourth-quarter earnings per share that fell short of estimates, although revenue matched forecasts. Looking ahead, the company predicted it would generate $6.50 billion and $6.90 billion in revenue this year, below analysts' consensus of $7.32 billion.

DraftKings 'Should Remain a Leader' in Betting Industry

In reaction, Jefferies analyst David Katz contended that the demand for U.S. sports bets remains high. Katz argued that DraftKings' conservative numbers were deliberate to absorb the upfront cost of the company's predictions product launch during the past quarter without accounting for revenue that could be generated.

The cautious guidance also considers the cost of developing DraftKings' ESPN Bet-branded sportsbook product and platforms, as well as the expansion into new U.S. states. As a result, Katz expects that DraftKings "should remain a leader" in the field.

Nonetheless, like other analysts, the Jefferies analyst trimmed his DKNG price target from $50 to $46, implying 111.40% upside.

Guggenheim Flags Robust Consumer Spending

Chipping in, Guggenheim analyst Curry Baker also lowered his DKNG price target from $42 to $37, implying 70.04% upside. Baker pointed out that DraftKings' revenue of $1.99 billion during the quarter fell below its estimate of $2.043 billion.

He argued that the figures were pressured by bets on the U.S. National Football League going heavily in favor of bettors, even as "underlying consumer demand for DraftKings products remains strong." The analyst sees the management as giving priority to cost management and improving efficiency.

"We expect DraftKings to remain active on the share repurchase front," Baker added.

Is DraftKings a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On Wall Street, DraftKings' shares remain a Strong Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 22 Buys and four Holds issued by 26 analysts over the past three months.

In addition, the average DKNG price target of $41.76 implies about 92% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: SBI Holdings Denies Holding $10 Billion in XRP Tokens, Says It Actually Owns 9% of Ripple Labs
data.description:
SBI Holdings (SBHGF) is setting the record straight regarding its relationship with Ripple and the XRP token (XRP-USD). Chairman Yoshitaka Kitao spoke out to address social media rumors claiming the Japanese financial giant held a massive $10 billion stash of XRP tokens. Kitao denied those reports, explaining that the company's real bet is on the equity of Ripple Labs itself. According to his statement, SBI owns roughly 9% of the company, a move that focuses on Ripple's business growth rather than just the price of the cryptocurrency.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SBI's Stake Is Worth a Fortune

While the firm isn't holding $10 billion in tokens, its 9% stake is still worth a fortune. Back in November 2025, Ripple reached a $40 billion valuation after a major investment round led by Citadel Securities and Fortress Investment Group. Based on those numbers, SBI's piece of the company is worth about $3.6 billion on paper. Kitao described this as a "hidden asset" because its true value could be much higher if Ripple's entire ecosystem, including its new stablecoin and payment networks, is taken into account.

Ripple CEO Aims for a $1 Trillion Future

The update from SBI comes at a time when Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse is making very big predictions. During a recent community event, Garlinghouse said he believes a crypto company will eventually reach a $1 trillion valuation, putting it in the same league as tech giants like Apple and Microsoft. He noted that Ripple has the "opportunity" to be that company if they successfully build out the XRP ecosystem. If Ripple ever hit that trillion-dollar mark, SBI's 9% stake would skyrocket to a staggering $90 billion.

XRP Price Struggles despite Big News

Even with these major institutional updates, the price of XRP has been having a hard time lately. The token dropped about 7.8% over the last 24 hours and is currently trading around $1.47. While Ripple is busy building global payment bridges and getting support from partners like SBI, the token price is still being dragged down by the broader crypto market.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.4784.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Are Chinese Tech Stocks Down Today? Alibaba (BABA) Leads Sell-Off
data.description:
Alibaba Group (BABA) (HK:9988), China's largest e-commerce and cloud computing company, led a drop in Chinese technology stocks after the U.S. Defense Department briefly placed several major firms on a military-linked companies list. The Chinese AI leader's stock slid more than 3% in Hong Kong, while Baidu (BIDU) (HK:9888) and BYD Co. (BYDDF) (HK:1211) were down about 1%.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Pentagon list named Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD as companies allegedly connected to China's military. The document was later marked "unpublished" and removed without explanation, but the move still unsettled investors and kept sentiment cautious.

Alibaba denied the allegation, saying it "is not a Chinese military company nor part of any military-civil fusion strategy," and added that it would take legal action against attempts to misrepresent the business. 

What This Means for Chinese Tech Stocks

When Tencent (TCEHY) and CATL (CYATY) were added to the list in 2021, their stocks dropped quickly. There were no fines or direct penalties, but the move created fear in the market. For instance, some investment funds stepped back, banks tightened compliance checks, and a few U.S. tech partnerships slowed. It showed that being named alone can create pressure, even without formal restrictions.

That's why investors are watching Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD closely now. All three are expanding in AI, cloud, robotics, and self-driving tech, areas already linked to U.S. export rules. If they are officially added to the list, partners and investors may step back until there is more clarity.

Which Chinese Stock Is the Best Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, all three stocks mentioned above show upside potential ahead. Alibaba has a Strong Buy consensus with about 30% upside, while BYD Company also carries a Strong Buy rating and roughly 28% upside. Baidu is likewise rated Strong Buy and offers about 24% upside.

Among them, Alibaba shows the highest expected return, though analysts remain broadly positive on all three names.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Feb. 15
data.description:
U.S. stocks ended the week lower as investors moved away from big tech and focused more on safer areas of the market. The S&P 500 (SPX) fell 1.28%, the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) dropped 1.27%, and the Dow (DJIA) slid 1.15%. data.title: 'I've Changed My Assumptions' – AI Is Coming for Office Work Faster Than Expected
data.description:
Anthropic's latest workplace push is adding a new layer to the AI story that investors are watching closely. Many firms have spent the past year building narrow AI tools for finance, law, and marketing. However, Anthropic is now offering a broader platform that could handle many of the same tasks in one place.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A recent Financial Times report described how Supercell used an AI analyst agent to produce a monthly report in five minutes instead of three hours. The company's finance lead said, "I was pretty bearish before starting this. I've changed my assumptions." That example shows how quickly routine office work is already shifting.

Anthropic's new tools, released under its Claude Cowork platform, are built to support tasks like contract review, forecasting, and customer support with less human input. The company says the goal is to make "the same powerful agent, but much more accessible." As a result, investors are starting to ask whether firms still need many separate niche AI products.

AI and the Next Wave of Office Job Pressure

This news also connects with a recent warning from Microsoft's (MSFT) AI chief, Mustafa Suleyman. In an interview with the Financial Times, he said AI will begin reshaping white-collar work in the next 12 to 18 months. This timeline suggests that profound change is not distant. Instead, it may arrive quickly across banking, legal work, sales, and media.

At the same time, this does not mean every worker or firm is replaced overnight. In many cases, AI is first taking over the repetitive work that is "least interesting," as Pigment's leadership described, while humans remain responsible for judgment and oversight.

What It Means for Investors and AI Stocks

For markets, the key issue is where value will build. Large platform firms may gain more leverage if they offer broad AI systems that reduce the need for many smaller subscriptions. Anthropic's move also raises questions for firms selling narrow tools on top of the big models.

On the other hand, industry leaders argue that specialists still matter. Legal AI providers point to the need for trust, audit trails, and strong data controls. One legal executive said, "Domain-specific tools only increase in value," because regulated fields require deeper checks than a general system can offer.

As Anthropic expands and Microsoft highlights the speed of change, the next year may bring sharper shifts in how companies spend on software, productivity, and staffing.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable publicly traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Claude. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Are the Markets Open Today, Washington's Birthday?
data.description:
The U.S. stock markets are closed today, February 16, 2026, in honor of Washington's Birthday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Keep track of all stock market holidays with TipRanks' Stock Market Holidays calendar.

Will the Holiday Effect Impact the Markets This Week?

Understanding how the stock market might be affected by the holiday will help you invest well and beat the markets. Investors sometimes refer to the pre-holiday market rise and the post-holiday price drift as the "holiday effect."

The holiday effect is linked to investors' general optimism and confidence. The fact that customers tend to spend more money around the holidays is one explanation for the pre-holiday market surge. The share prices of retailers, in particular, may increase as a result.

As for the post-holiday price drift, one reason for it is that many traders and market analysts take time off work during the holidays to relax from their regular trading and investment research schedules. Those investors view the holidays as a time to unwind and refresh. Alternatively, some traders see the season as a chance to work harder and get some tactical advantages in the market, maybe by taking advantage of the period when many other traders take time off.

Read TipRanks News to learn about Washington's Birthday holiday effect on the stock market. And remember to always use TipRanks to stay up-to-date on your stock research.
